Can SHAPE data discriminate RNA 3D structures?

CASP17 target R2307 (reference: FARFAR2 Mol9_reference_UtoG_buildloop). Decoys: 15 FARFAR2 + 197 CASP17 submitted models.

Discrimination summary (Spearman ρ vs RMSD)

More negative ρ = better discrimination (lower RMSD → higher SHAPE agreement).

MetricSpearman ρ
Naive SHAPE (vs experiment) vs RMSD-0.512
SGNM SHAPE (vs experiment) vs RMSD-0.413
Naive SHAPE (vs ref model) vs RMSD-0.501
SGNM SHAPE (vs ref model) vs RMSD-0.522
Base-pair F1 vs RMSD-0.579

SHAPE score vs RMSD & F1 — vs EXPERIMENTAL data

Predicted SHAPE correlated to experimental 2A3 SHAPE (OpenKnot gRNAde P20). The reference (true) structure is the black star — it sits near the top at its real correlation (~0.6), not 1.0, confirming the true fold best matches experiment.

SHAPE score vs RMSD & F1 — vs REFERENCE-MODEL simulated data

Predicted SHAPE correlated to the reference-model simulated SHAPE (ceiling analysis). The reference trivially sits at correlation = 1.0 (black star).

Structural-accuracy consistency: base-pair F1 vs RMSD

Two structural-accuracy measures agree (lower RMSD → higher F1).

Do the two SHAPE predictors agree?

SGNM-predicted vs naive paired/unpaired SHAPE correlation, for both experimental and reference-model targets.

Secondary-structure SHAPE proxy heatmap

Unique DSSR secondary structures (dot-bracket on each cell), white=paired, red=unpaired, sorted by correlation to the reference.

SGNM-predicted SHAPE profile heatmap

Continuous SGNM-predicted reactivity (per-row min-max normalized), sorted by correlation to the reference-model profile.

Methods

  • RMSD: C1' RMSD to reference via TMscore.
  • Naive SHAPE: DSSR paired/unpaired (0/0.5), Pearson r to reference pattern.
  • SGNM SHAPE: hmblair/sgnm GNM model predicts per-residue reactivity from 3D structure; Pearson r of each model's profile to the reference-model profile. (The stronger equivariant model needs CUDA; run on a GPU node for phase 3.)
  • Base-pair F1: F1 of DSSR base pairs vs reference.

Note: experimental SHAPE comparison pending real per-nucleotide data; current "vs reference" analysis establishes the ceiling on discrimination power.
